seoTitle: Create a Custom Invoice Template in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Build a branded invoice template in QuickBooks Online, from uploading your logo to customizing colors, content, and email delivery settings.
intro: A generic invoice can undercut an otherwise professional business. This guide shows how to use QuickBooks Online's Custom Form Styles tool to build an invoice template that reflects your brand, from logo placement to column layout, so every bill you send looks polished.

steps.heading: Open Custom Form Styles
steps.body: Click the gear icon in the top right of QuickBooks Online and select Custom Form Styles to reach the template directory.

steps.heading: Start a new invoice style
steps.body: Choose New style, select Invoice, and pick a layout base to work from in the Design tab.

steps.heading: Add your branding
steps.body: Upload your company logo, align it on the invoice, and choose a color palette and font that match your brand identity.

steps.heading: Edit content columns
steps.body: Use the Content tab to adjust header fields, rearrange table columns, and rewrite default labels so they fit your industry.

steps.heading: Configure emails and save
steps.body: Set how the invoice summary appears in client inboxes under the Emails tab, then preview the live PDF and click Done to lock in your template.

faq.question: Can I have more than one invoice template?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online lets you create and save multiple custom styles and choose which one to apply to each invoice.

faq.question: Will editing a template change past invoices?
faq.answer: No, template changes apply going forward; previously sent invoices keep the layout they were created with.

faq.question: Can I remove unused columns from the invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, in the Content tab you can uncheck rows you don't need, such as unused product/service columns.

faq.question: Is it possible to add a custom footer or thank-you note?
faq.answer: Yes, the footer section of the form editor lets you add custom text like late fee terms or a personalized message.
